mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or null Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase creates supply bottlenecks and inflationary pressures, prompting market participants to price in tighter monetary policy and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the mechanism fails to produce a statistically significant negative Rank IC in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 3.5 percent signals persistent inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 70% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a robust labor market that underpins wage growth and consumer spending, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal generates a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong real GDP growth and low inflation creates a Goldilocks environment that maximizes corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month when both conditions hold simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth crosses above 2.0 and CPIAUCSL crosses below 3.0
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or null Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher than baseline when the dual condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO falls by more than 0.5 percent, or the signal fails to achieve a negative Rank IC > 0.03 in walk-forward validation.
